---
title: "Financial Options and Monte Carlo"
author: "Trevor Isaacson, Noah Smith and Buzz Walter"
date: "12/7/2021"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(rmarkdown)
library(ggplot2)
library(dbplyr)
```

Monte Carlo integration is a statistical computing method used to approximate integrals based on random sampling.  Monte Carlo simulations are computer driven simulations that generate a large number of samples from a distribution that is characterized by the population of which the samples are being drawn from.  


These simulations can be used in many different areas and fields including finance, engineering, supply chain and many others.
